title: Epilasia cenopleura
taxonomy: Epilasia cenopleura is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a terrestrial orchid native to New Guinea. This orchid is also known as the "Candy Orchid" due to its unique and colorful appearance. Epilasia cenopleura is part of the subtribe Gynostemmatinae, which includes other terrestrial orchids. The classification of Epilasia cenopleura is further divided into the genus Epilasia and the species E. cenopleura.

intro: Epilasia cenopleura, also known as the "Satin Pothos" or "Mirror-Leaf", is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. Traditionally, Epilasia cenopleura has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and inflammation, due to its potential antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include iridoid glycosides, such as epilasioside and loganioid glycosides, which have been shown to possess pharmacological activities. Epilasia cenopleura is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to alleviate respiratory issues, fever, and other health concerns.
